subagent_reports.topic: Temporal signal payload + history limits for content-in-state migration

sources.label: Temporal docs — Troubleshoot payload and gRPC message size limit errors
sources.summary: Per-payload limit is 2 MB on Temporal Cloud; default 2 MB on self-hosted (configurable). gRPC message limit is 4 MB per request — applies to combined commands+payloads in a single Workflow Task. Signals are payloads and subject to the 2 MB cap.

sources.label: Temporal Cloud — System limits
sources.summary: Cloud: 2 MB payload (non-configurable), 4 MB per gRPC message, 4 MB per Event History transaction. Per-Workflow-Execution limit: max 10,000 Signals; once reached, no more Signals are processed.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Events and Event History limits
sources.summary: Terminate at 51,200 Events, >2000 Updates, or >10,000 Signals. Warning emitted at 10,240 Events. Continue-As-New is the official mitigation.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Errors (Bad Signal Input Size, gRPC Message Too Large)
sources.summary: Oversize signal payload → 'Bad Signal Input Size'. gRPC Message Too Large terminates the workflow (non-recoverable). Mitigations: smaller batches, payload codec compression, continue-as-new.

sources.label: Temporal blog — Managing very long-running Workflows
sources.summary: Hard limits: 51,200 Events / 50 MB. Warnings start at 10,240 Events. Must drain pending Signals before continueAsNew or risk termination near the limit. Recommendation: continueAsNew well before the cap.

sources.label: Temporal community — official recommended size targets
sources.summary: Temporal staff (Samar) recommendation: keep history length <10K events AND history size <10 MB. Server config knobs: HistorySizeLimitWarn=10MB, HistorySizeLimitError=50MB.

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK docs — Continue-As-New
sources.summary: Use `wf.workflowInfo().continueAsNewSuggested` (server-driven hint). New run gets a fresh Event History but same Workflow ID; state must be passed explicitly as input.

sources.label: eslint-plugin-temporal rule — continueAsNew must pass state
sources.summary: continueAsNew does NOT auto-carry in-memory state. All preserved state must be explicit in the next-run input; the state becomes the new run's first WorkflowExecutionStartedEvent payload (subject to the 2 MB payload limit).

sources.label: xgrid — Continue-As-New trigger guidance
sources.summary: Practical trigger: history length 2,000–5,000 events depending on per-event payload size. Use historyLength, not wall-clock time.

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK source — signal/update handler dispatch contract
sources.summary: Activation jobs processed Signals → Updates → other. Within each group, the SDK preserves the order accepted by the server. Signals dispatched to handlers in server-acceptance order. If handler is async, runs to its first await/yield.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Task Queues / ordering
sources.summary: Event order within a Workflow Execution History is guaranteed constant once written. Signal Events are part of that History.

sources.label: sdk-core issue #640 — signal/update reordering
sources.summary: SDK may reorder signals relative to other activation jobs (timers/conditions) but preserves server-acceptance order among signals of the same name. Late-handler-registration is the main reordering driver.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Versioning / Safe deployments
sources.summary: Adding NEW signal handlers + NEW optional state fields is replay-safe: replay determinism is governed by Command-producing API order (activities, timers, child workflows, awaits). Reading new optional fields does not introduce commands. Patching only required when await-order or command-producing logic changes.

sources.label: Temporal blog — robust message handlers
sources.summary: allHandlersFinished + handler-unfinished policy. Drain handlers before continueAsNew to avoid dangling/lost work — ADV's `await wf.condition(wf.allHandlersFinished)` already follows this pattern.

sources.label: Temporal docs — default workflow retention (self-hosted source)
sources.summary: Self-hosted defaultWorkflowRetention = 1 day. Namespace-level WorkflowExecutionRetentionTtl overrides it. Retention timer applies at workflow close time.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Archival
sources.summary: On close, retention starts; closed execution data deleted from primary persistence after retention TTL. Archival (if enabled) ships history to blob storage prior to deletion.

sources.label: Temporal Cloud — Serverless / retention
sources.summary: Temporal Cloud retains closed-workflow histories up to a 90-day retention window (configurable). Self-hosted default minimum is 1 day.

sources.label: ADV repo — shouldContinueAsNewFromInfo + threshold
sources.summary: DEFAULT_CHANGE_HISTORY_THRESHOLD = 5,000 events. Helper: returns true if `continueAsNewSuggested` is true OR `historyLength >= 5000`. Override via ADV_TEMPORAL_CHANGE_HISTORY_THRESHOLD env. Already aligns with Temporal's recommendation of <10K events.

sources.label: ADV repo — continueAsNew drain + seed pattern
sources.summary: Workflow calls `await wf.condition(wf.allHandlersFinished)` before `continueAsNew<typeof changeWorkflow>(seed)`. The seed includes `state.documents` (already part of state today).

sources.label: ADV repo — existing document signal handlers
sources.summary: `proposalUpdatedSignal`, `problemStatementUpdatedSignal`, `agreementUpdatedSignal`, `designUpdatedSignal` already defined with DocumentUpdate-style payloads. Adding `executiveSummaryUpdatedSignal` + `acceptanceUpdatedSignal` is structurally identical to existing signals.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment:
PROPOSED CAPS (64 KB soft warn / 256 KB hard reject) ARE SOUND AND CONSERVATIVE.

1. Per-signal payload limit (Q1): Temporal Cloud enforces 2 MB per payload (non-configurable); self-hosted default is 2 MB (configurable). The 256 KB hard reject is ~12.5% of the 2 MB ceiling — leaves substantial headroom for JSON serialization framing, metadata fields (commit_sha, ts, source_path, etc.), and Protobuf payload encoding overhead. 64 KB soft is well below any per-payload concern; it is a UX/quality guardrail, not a Temporal-imposed limit. VALIDATED.

2. Workflow history limits (Q2): Hard caps are 51,200 Events AND 50 MB AND 10,000 Signals per workflow execution (https://docs.temporal.io/workflow-execution/event, https://docs.temporal.io/cloud/limits). Server warns at 10,240 events / 10 MB. Official Temporal staff recommendation: keep <10K events AND <10 MB history size. ADV's DEFAULT_CHANGE_HISTORY_THRESHOLD = 5,000 events is well within best practice. continueAsNewSuggested + threshold OR is the canonical pattern (matches the docs snippet exactly). VALIDATED — no change needed.

3. continueAsNew state accumulation (Q3): continueAsNew does NOT auto-carry in-memory state. The seed (passed as workflow input) becomes the first WorkflowExecutionStartedEvent payload — itself subject to the 2 MB per-payload limit. With 6 × 50 KB docs = 300 KB documents + the rest of ChangeWorkflowState (tasks, contract, wisdom, etc.) the seed is comfortably under 2 MB. ACCUMULATION across cycles is NOT a concern — each continueAsNew produces a FRESH history; previous-run history is not carried into the new run. Per-cycle overhead is bounded by current state size, not cumulative. The dominant risk is the seed approaching 2 MB on a single transition if state.documents grows beyond ~1.5 MB total. With 256 KB hard cap × 6 docs = 1.5 MB worst case for documents alone — leaves only ~500 KB headroom for the rest of state. CAUTION: if all 6 artifacts hit the hard cap simultaneously, seed payload approaches the 2 MB ceiling.

4. Additive optional state fields (Q4): Adding `executiveSummary?` and `acceptance?` to `state.documents` is REPLAY-SAFE. Replay determinism is governed by the order of Command-producing APIs (activities, timers, child workflows, awaits). Reading/writing optional state fields produces no commands; old histories with no events for these fields replay cleanly because the fields stay `undefined`. (https://docs.temporal.io/develop/safe-deployments). VALIDATED.

5. Additive signal handlers (Q5): Adding new signal handlers (`executiveSummaryUpdatedSignal`, `acceptanceUpdatedSignal`) is REPLAY-SAFE for existing histories. Signal events only appear in histories where the signal was actually fired; replay of pre-existing histories simply will not encounter these signal events. SDK behavior: handlers registered via `setHandler` for signal names not present in history are inert during replay. No patching required. (Same source as Q4.) VALIDATED.

6. Signal ordering (Q6): Temporal TS SDK preserves SERVER-ACCEPTANCE ORDER for signals of the same name AND for signals across names that arrive in the same activation. The SDK source explicitly states: 'Activation jobs are processed in the order supplied by sdk-core, i.e. Signals, then Updates, then other jobs. Within each group, the order sent by the server is preserved.' (sdk-typescript/packages/workflow/src/workflow.ts). HOWEVER: 'server-acceptance order' is NOT 'tool-fire order' — the server orders by arrival, and network/transport may slightly reorder concurrent in-flight signals. With SEQUENTIAL awaits (tool fires signal 1, awaits ack, fires signal 2, awaits ack, ...), server-acceptance order is guaranteed to match fire order. With concurrent/Promise.all firing, order is NOT guaranteed. ADV's proposed pattern of 'sequential fire in deterministic order' is the CORRECT pattern. VALIDATED with caveat: sequential awaits required.

7. Retention (Q7): Self-hosted default `WorkflowExecutionRetentionTtl` = 1 day; Temporal Cloud default up to 90 days. After retention expires, the workflow history (including state.documents) is DELETED from primary persistence. Archival (if enabled) ships to blob storage first. ADV's git-committed archive bundle is the durable record — Temporal state is treated as ephemeral. This aligns with ADV's existing model (downstream projection to disk on terminal/gate transitions). CAUTION: after archive, if the user re-runs adv tools against an old change-id whose workflow history has expired, document content will only be available from the git bundle, not from Temporal state. ADV must read from disk after archival regardless. VALIDATED — no architectural change required.

ADDITIONAL OBSERVATIONS:
- Per-Workflow-Execution Signal cap is 10,000 (Temporal Cloud). Each artifact update fires 1 signal. With 6 artifacts × maybe 20–50 updates each over a change lifetime, expected signal count per change is <500. Plenty of headroom; continueAsNew resets this counter.
- gRPC 4 MB request cap applies to a single Workflow Task response (commands+payloads). Even with 6 sequential signal applications in one task, total stays comfortably under 4 MB at 256 KB per artifact.
- ADV already drains handlers (`await wf.condition(wf.allHandlersFinished)`) before continueAsNew — matches the canonical guidance.

validation.notes: Q1 PASS — 256 KB hard / 64 KB soft well below Temporal's 2 MB per-payload limit on both Cloud and self-hosted. Q2 PASS — ADV's 5,000-event continueAsNew threshold sits well below the 10K warning and 51.2K hard limit; matches Temporal staff guidance. Q3 PASS WITH CAUTION — worst-case 6 × 256 KB = 1.5 MB documents leaves only ~500 KB seed headroom; recommend tracking total documents size against a 1.5 MB aggregate soft warn. Q4 PASS — optional additive fields are replay-safe. Q5 PASS — additive signal handlers are replay-safe for histories that never received the new signals. Q6 PASS — sequential awaits in tool layer guarantee server-acceptance order matches fire order; concurrent firing would not. Q7 PASS — Temporal state is ephemeral after retention TTL; ADV's git-committed archive bundle is the durable record (existing model).

subagent_reports.recommendation:
CONFIRM agreement caps and constraints AS PROPOSED, with two refinements:

1. AC11 (per-artifact size caps) — KEEP 64 KB soft warn / 256 KB hard reject. Both are safely below the 2 MB Temporal payload limit. Cite the 2 MB ceiling explicitly in the agreement so reviewers understand the headroom rationale.

2. ADD an aggregate documents-size invariant to AC11 or as a new AC: soft warn at ~1 MB total across all 6 documents, hard reject before total reaches ~1.8 MB. Rationale: continueAsNew seed is itself a payload subject to the 2 MB limit, and must also carry tasks/contract/wisdom/etc. Worst-case 6 × 256 KB = 1.5 MB documents alone leaves only ~500 KB for the rest of ChangeWorkflowState. An aggregate guard prevents continueAsNew from failing on seed payload overflow. The aggregate check happens in the tool layer before firing a signal that would push total over the limit.

3. C3 (history size constraint) — KEEP 'stay well under 50 MB'. ADV's existing 5,000-event continueAsNew threshold already enforces this. NO change required. Cite the official Temporal recommendation of <10K events / <10 MB.

4. C4 (signal ordering) — KEEP 'sequential fire in deterministic order'. CLARIFY in the agreement: the tool layer MUST `await` each signal RPC ack before firing the next. Do NOT use `Promise.all` or unawaited fires. The SDK preserves server-acceptance order; sequential awaits guarantee server-acceptance order matches fire order. Document this contract explicitly so future contributors don't 'optimize' it into parallel firing.

5. C6 (crash-recovery) — KEEP 're-issue mid-batch on failure'. Signals are at-least-once with idempotent state-replacement semantics (the signal payload IS the new content; re-applying produces the same state). Recommend the tool layer treat each signal fire as idempotent and safe to retry on transient error. Document that mid-batch failure (e.g. signal 4 of 6 fails) leaves state.documents in a partial state — the tool layer should re-fire the failed signal AND every subsequent one in the batch to preserve ordering invariant.

6. ADDITIVE replay safety — NO patching required for adding `executiveSummary?` / `acceptance?` state fields or their signal handlers. Old histories replay cleanly because (a) optional fields produce no commands during reads, and (b) signal handlers for un-fired signals are inert during replay. The existing workflow-bundle-boundary test in plugin/src/temporal/workflows.test.ts will catch any accidental command-producing additions. Recommend the agreement explicitly state 'no patching required' so reviewers don't second-guess.

7. RETENTION semantic — document in agreement that Temporal state.documents is ephemeral (subject to namespace retention TTL after archive). The archive bundle remains the durable record. ADV tools that read content post-archive must read from disk, not from workflow state. This is already ADV's existing model; just make it explicit.
follow_ups: Consider exposing documents aggregate size as a search attribute or query for operational visibility (e.g. surface 'change X has 1.4 MB of artifact content' in adv_status). Out of scope for this change but useful for capacity planning.

Verify Temporal namespace retention TTL ADV uses in production / dev. If self-hosted at default 1-day retention, the 'read from disk after archive' fallback is critical — confirm this code path exists in adv_change_show post-archive.

Evaluate whether ADV should adopt Worker Versioning or patching policy for any FUTURE non-additive changes to document signal payload schemas. Current change is additive-only, so no patching required; future schema migrations on existing signals (e.g. adding a required field to DocumentUpdateBaseSchema) would need patching.

Consider whether a per-change signal-count budget metric (current Cloud cap = 10K signals/execution, reset by continueAsNew) should be tracked. With 6 artifacts × typical ~10 revisions = 60 signals per change-cycle, well within budget — but worth a sanity check in adv_status.

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for removePositionalArtifactApi against approved contract

sources.label: Temporal docs — Handling Signals, Queries & Updates (exceptions section)
sources.summary: Authoritative behavior of throw in signal vs update handlers. (1) ApplicationFailure thrown from a Signal handler fails the ENTIRE workflow. (2) Any other exception causes a Workflow Task Failure → workflow gets stuck retrying the handler until code change. (3) Only Update handlers can throw to reject while keeping the workflow running. Update Validators (read-only) are the canonical rejection mechanism.

sources.label: Temporal Failures reference
sources.summary: Confirms ApplicationFailure with nonRetryable causes Workflow Execution Failure when thrown from workflow code; non-Temporal exceptions cause Workflow Task Failure retried indefinitely.

sources.label: Temporal TS SDK API — Updates/Signals/Queries
sources.summary: Signal handlers return void (no client visibility for rejections); Update handlers return values and may be rejected via Validator. Validator is the recommended pre-acceptance reject path.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Safe Deployments (additive change replay)
sources.summary: Additive optional state fields and additive signal handlers are replay-safe. Aligns with C7 and design's additive-only approach.

sources.label: ADV workflows.ts — safeUpdateHandler
sources.summary: Existing wrapper used for ALL signal handlers; catches throw and re-throws as wf.ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable. Per Temporal docs this fails the workflow for signal callers. Comment at line 378-379 even acknowledges 'Signal callers do not observe returns; signal handlers should prefer local stuck-state handling before throw.' Pre-existing project pattern — but KD-8 layer 2 doubles down on it.

sources.label: ADV workflows.ts — existing pattern for rejecting at signal time (applyGateStuckToState)
sources.summary: Canonical ADV pattern for in-handler rejection: mutate state into a 'stuck' status rather than throw. Marks the gate as readiness-blocked and continues processing. This is the LBP for signal rejection in ADV codebase.

sources.label: ADV workflow-start.ts — ensureChangeWorkflowStarted
sources.summary: Cold-vs-warm detection is exception-driven (catches WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStarted). seedState is consumed only at start; subsequent getHandle() reuses. KD-5 hydration must run BEFORE client.workflow.start() call, NOT after — design sketch (lines 122-132) is structurally compatible but logically reversed (mutates input.seedState before the try block, not in a 'cold-start branch').

sources.label: ADV activities.ts — inspectArtifactActivity
sources.summary: Activity that reads disk artifact metadata. KD-5 proposes calling it during workflow START (caller side, not workflow side). This is fine because workflow-start.ts is client-side caller code, not workflow code — activities can be invoked here as ordinary async functions. Confirmed by inspection: activities.ts has no @temporal/workflow imports; it is plain Node code.

sources.label: Temporal docs — Workflow Initializers / messages before main
sources.summary: Messages can arrive before the workflow's main body runs (signal-with-start, backlogged task queues, post-continueAsNew). 'In Go and TypeScript, register any message handlers only after completing initialization.' Relevant to KD-5 hydration ordering: if hydration runs in workflow start input.seedState, the workflow's main body must apply seedState.documents BEFORE setHandler() for content signals, otherwise an in-flight signal can race the hydration.

sources.label: Temporal community — signal ordering within activation
sources.locator: https://community.temporal.io (cited via existing research provenance, May 28 2026)
sources.summary: TS SDK preserves server-acceptance order within a single activation. Sequential await IS required for ordering guarantees (Promise.all reorders). Validates KD-3/KD-4.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment:
## Contract Compliance Audit (12 ACs + 15 constraints + 8 avoidances = 35 items)

### Acceptance Criteria

- AC1 (source of truth): SATISFIED by KD-6 readArtifact + KD-4 fan-out writing state.documents.
- AC2 (cross-session isolation): SATISFIED by AC1 mechanism (Temporal-backed reads work regardless of disk).
- AC3 (typed payload API): SATISFIED by KD-2 + KD-12 compile-time invariants.
- AC4 (unified ArtifactKind): SATISFIED by KD-1 + KD-12 type lock + deletes listed in Module Surface.
- AC5 (six-artifact coverage): SATISFIED by KD-2 (6-field schema) + KD-3 (6-tuple array).
- AC6 (signal invariant): SATISFIED by KD-12 artifact-payload-signal-invariant.test.ts + KD-4 'continue when undefined' branch.
- AC7 (consumer alignment): SATISFIED — consumers already read state.documents (validated in proposal); KD-4 writes activate them.
- AC8 (no disk writes from temporal store): SATISFIED by KD-9 separation + phase 16 removal.
- AC9 (workflow-start hydration): SATISFIED in PRINCIPLE by KD-5 but with structural concerns — see Open Q3 below.
- AC10 (archive bundle materialization): NOT directly addressed by any KD — design mentions consumers light up automatically (KD-7 row archive-summary) but the archive-bundle WRITE path that materializes 6 files into .adv/archive/{cid}-{ts}/ is not explicitly redesigned. GAP-1 (minor): design should cite which archive activity reads state.documents and writes the bundle.
- AC11 (tool surface parity): SATISFIED by C10 → KD-2 (internal store API only).
- AC12 (full lifecycle integration): SATISFIED by KD-12 full-lifecycle.itest.ts.

### Constraints

- C1 (scope): SATISFIED (KD list touches only artifact markdown).
- C2 (archive bundle format unchanged): SATISFIED via KD-9 reasoning, but tied to GAP-1.
- C3 (per-artifact size caps): SATISFIED by KD-8 layer 1 (tool pre-check) but Layer 2 mechanism is problematic — see CAUTION-1.
- C4 (aggregate caps): SATISFIED by KD-8 validateAggregateSize.
- C5 (deterministic signal ordering, sequential await): SATISFIED by KD-3 + KD-4.
- C6 (backward compat): SATISFIED by KD-5 hydration (subject to Open Q3 concerns).
- C7 (replay safety): SATISFIED by additive-only approach.
- C8 (crash-recovery): SATISFIED by KD-4 idempotent state-replacement semantics; covering test in KD-12.
- C9 (batched read): SATISFIED by KD-6 readArtifacts batched query.
- C10 (tool surface unchanged): SATISFIED.
- C11 (no new storage abstraction): SATISFIED.
- C12 (acceptance recovery retains disk): SATISFIED by KD-9 dual-store coexistence.
- C13 (atomic removal): SATISFIED by KD-10 phase 17.
- C14 (worktree isolation): SATISFIED (process-level).
- C15 (structural correctness): PARTIALLY SATISFIED — KD-8 layer 2 intent is correct but mechanism is wrong (CAUTION-1). Use state-mutation rejection (mark stuck), not throw.

### Avoidances
- A1-A8: SATISFIED — design does not touch out-of-scope surfaces.

## Per-KD Soundness

- KD-1 (canonical types file): SOUND. Mirrors existing types/gates.ts pattern.
- KD-2 (Zod schema): SOUND. Mirrors DocumentUpdateBaseSchema pattern.
- KD-3 (explicit ordered array): SOUND and structural per P33. Idiomatic for TS SDK signal fan-out: TS SDK has no batched-signal primitive; explicit ordered tuples + sequential await is the canonical pattern (no library convention to choose between).
- KD-4 (sequential await): SOUND. Matches Temporal TS SDK requirement (per-activation ordering preserved only when serial).
- KD-5 (workflow-start hydration): MOSTLY SOUND but three structural concerns (see Open Q3):
  (a) Hydration must run on the CALLER side (workflow-start.ts) BEFORE client.workflow.start(), not inside the workflow. Design says 'inside ensureChangeWorkflowStarted, cold-start branch' which is workflow-start.ts caller code — OK if that's the intent.
  (b) Cold/warm detection is exception-driven (catch WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStarted). To avoid wasted disk I/O on warm path, hydration should be lazy/deferred until after the start attempt succeeds, or use a separate workflow-existence probe (extra RPC). Design as written reads disk every call, then conditionally writes seedState — wasted I/O on warm path. Minor performance issue.
  (c) Partial-disk-write robustness: if a previous run crashed mid-write leaving e.g. proposal.md but truncated agreement.md, inspectArtifactActivity returns whatever bytes are on disk. Hydration would seed truncated content. The disk write is not transactionally guaranteed atomic at the FS level (write-then-rename is not used by legacy.changes per typical Node fs.writeFile semantics). Mitigation: contentHash verification in state.artifacts.{kind} — if hash mismatch on hydration, skip that artifact and log warning.
  (d) Concurrent workflow-start race: Temporal's WorkflowIdReusePolicy at the server level guarantees only one workflow exists per workflowId; ensureChangeWorkflowStarted handles the WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStarted race. If two callers race, the loser falls into the warm path and gets the existing handle — does NOT re-hydrate. SAFE.
- KD-6 (unified readArtifact): SOUND. Matches C9.
- KD-7 (read callsite migration): SOUND. Mechanical.
- KD-8 (size cap enforcement): Layer 1 SOUND. **Layer 2 contains a factually incorrect comment and an architectural misuse of Temporal signal semantics** — see CAUTION-1.
- KD-9 (dual-store retention): SOUND. Necessary for C12.
- KD-10 (migration phase ordering): SOUND. Writes-before-reads is correct for in-flight workflow safety (in-flight workflows under old code keep reading from disk; new write path populates state.documents; once read path migrates, both old and new in-flight workflows still work because readArtifact falls back to archive/disk for empty state.documents). NO deployment-safety concern under the additive replay-safe contract (C7). Old workflows replaying under new code see new signal-handler registrations but old histories without those signals — additive registration is replay-safe per Temporal docs.
- KD-11 (audit table): GOOD — explicit per-constraint mapping.
- KD-12 (test architecture): SOUND. Compile-time invariant + recording-signal-client + replay test covers AC6, AC9, C7.

## Open Question Answers

### Q1: Is explicit ordered-array (KD-3) idiomatic for TS SDK, or should we use single updateDocumentsSignal?

**ANSWER: Explicit ordered array is correct and idiomatic. Do NOT batch into a single signal.** Rationale:

1. Temporal TS SDK has no 'batched signal' primitive. Both patterns are equally supported at SDK level.
2. A7 in the agreement explicitly forbids batching as out of scope ('preserves existing pattern, one signal type per artifact kind, matches existing reducer architecture'). Batching would violate the approved contract.
3. Existing ADV pattern is one signal per content kind (proposalUpdatedSignal, problemStatementUpdatedSignal, ... — workflows.ts:893-922 already wires 4 of these); the 2 new ones (executiveSummaryUpdatedSignal, acceptanceUpdatedSignal) follow the same shape. Consistency wins.
4. Per-signal payload size cap (256 KB hard) is well under 2 MB per-payload Temporal limit; a single batched signal could approach the 2 MB cap if all 6 artifacts are at max size (6 × 256 KB = 1.5 MB before serialization overhead) — uncomfortably close.
5. Replay safety: 6 separate signal types with additive handlers replay cleanly. A single batched signal forces a new union-type payload schema; history events with old single-artifact payloads would need a discriminator branch.

KD-3 is right. Keep the explicit ordered array. (Note: KD-3 array references signals like `proposalUpdatedSignal` etc. — verify in implementation that all 6 signal constants are defined in messages.ts including the 2 new ones for executiveSummary and acceptance.)

### Q2: Is throw in signal handler (KD-8 layer 2) correct, or should we use ApplicationFailure with nonRetryable: true?

**ANSWER: NEITHER is correct. Both are wrong per Temporal docs.** This is the most serious finding.

Per https://docs.temporal.io/handling-messages#exceptions:
- Throwing **ApplicationFailure from a signal handler fails the ENTIRE workflow execution**.
- Throwing any other exception from a signal handler causes a **Workflow Task Failure**, which puts the workflow into an infinite retry loop on the bad signal until code is changed.

The design comment at line 217 — 'rejects signal, workflow continues' — is factually wrong. Both throw paths are workflow-fatal or workflow-stuck. KD-8 Layer 2 would actively destroy workflows on size-cap violations rather than reject them.

**Correct mechanisms** (in order of preference):

1. **State-mutation rejection (LBP for ADV).** Match the existing `applyGateStuckToState` pattern (workflows.ts:722-732, 1098). On size violation, mutate state.documents.{kind} to a 'rejected' marker or set a state.artifacts.{kind}.rejection field, log via wf.log.warn, and DO NOT mutate state.documents.{kind} to the oversized content. The workflow continues; the next read shows the artifact was not updated; the caller (tool layer pre-check, Layer 1) is the primary enforcement and Layer 2 is a defensive no-op that records the violation in workflow state without poisoning.
2. **Move enforcement to client-side (caller of handle.signal)**. Layer 1 already does this. Layer 2 is redundant defense-in-depth; if you must keep it, make it a structural state-mutation rejection.
3. **Use an Update instead of a Signal for size-validated writes**. Updates support validators that throw to reject WITHOUT failing the workflow. This is the canonical Temporal answer for 'I need to reject a write at the server'. BUT this is a much larger architectural change (signals → updates migration) and likely outside scope.

**Recommended fix to design**: Rewrite KD-8 Layer 2 as:
```ts
wf.setHandler(proposalUpdatedSignal, signalMutation('proposalUpdated', (payload) => {
  if (Buffer.byteLength(payload.text, 'utf-8') > ARTIFACT_HARD_CAP) {
    wf.log.warn('artifact-size-cap-violation', { kind: 'proposal', size: ... });
    state.artifacts.proposal = { ...state.artifacts.proposal, rejection: { reason: 'size-cap', size, cap: ARTIFACT_HARD_CAP, at: payload.updatedAt } };
    return state; // no document mutation
  }
  return applyProposalUpdatedToState(state, payload);
}));
```
This preserves structural-correctness intent (P33, C15) without poisoning the workflow.

**Caveat**: The existing safeUpdateHandler wrapper (workflows.ts:350) ALSO has this issue project-wide — it re-throws ALL signal-handler errors as ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable. This is a pre-existing latent bug. Outside this change's scope, but worth flagging as a follow-up.

### Q3: Is workflow-start hydration (KD-5) robust against partial disk writes / concurrent starts?

**ANSWER: Mostly robust, with three caveats:**

(a) **Concurrent starts**: SAFE. Temporal server WorkflowIdReusePolicy guarantees one workflow per workflowId; the loser's `client.workflow.start()` call throws WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStarted and falls into `getHandle` (warm path) which does NOT re-hydrate. Confirmed by reading workflow-start.ts:39-65.

(b) **Partial-write robustness**: WEAK. If a previous crash left e.g. proposal.md fully written but agreement.md truncated mid-fsync, inspectArtifactActivity will return whatever bytes are on disk. Hydration seeds truncated content into state.documents, which then becomes the source of truth. Mitigations:
 - The existing change.json + state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash pairing means we can verify: read disk artifact, compute sha256, compare to state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash if present. If mismatch, skip hydration for that kind and log warning. (Note: pre-migration changes may not have contentHash recorded — in that case, hydrate without verification and accept the risk; same risk that existed before this change.)
 - Document in C8 crash-recovery semantics that partial-write recovery is best-effort; users may need to re-issue updateArtifacts to overwrite truncated content. The KD-4 idempotent overwrite path supports this.

(c) **Workflow main-body ordering**: KD-5 hydrates via seedState.documents passed at start. The workflow body (workflows.ts changeWorkflow function) must consume seedState.documents BEFORE setHandler() calls for content signals — otherwise a signal arriving between worker pickup and main-body init could race the hydration. Existing workflow init in workflows.ts already applies seedState fields before setHandler registration; just ensure seedState.documents is one of the fields restored before setHandler(proposalUpdatedSignal, ...).

Design is functionally sound. Add (i) contentHash verification during hydration when available, (ii) explicit assertion that seedState.documents is restored before content-signal handler registration in workflow body, (iii) document partial-write best-effort behavior in temporal-recovery.md.

## Migration Phase Ordering Verdict (KD-10)

SOUND. Writes-before-reads is the correct order. In-flight workflow safety:
- Old workflows running under old code: keep reading from disk via legacy.changes — no impact.
- Old workflows replaying under new code after restart: new signal-handler registrations are additive (replay-safe per Temporal safe-deployments doc); old histories without state.documents events replay cleanly with state.documents = {} default. Subsequent updateArtifacts calls populate state.documents. Next read returns Temporal-backed.
- New workflows: state.documents populated from cold-start signals.

No replay-cliff risk. Phase ordering is correct.

blockers: KD-8 Layer 2 (signal-handler throw for size-cap rejection) is architecturally incorrect per Temporal docs (https://docs.temporal.io/handling-messages#exceptions). Throwing ApplicationFailure from a signal handler fails the ENTIRE workflow; throwing any other exception causes a Workflow Task Failure (stuck workflow). The design comment 'rejects signal, workflow continues' is factually wrong. As written, the design would cause Temporal to destroy workflows on size-cap violations instead of rejecting the bad write. Fix before design gate completion: replace throw with state-mutation rejection (mark state.artifacts.{kind}.rejection, log, continue) matching the existing applyGateStuckToState pattern.
validation.notes:
1. AC10 (archive bundle materialization) lacks an explicit KD mapping — design should cite which archive activity reads state.documents and writes the 6 files into .adv/archive/{cid}-{ts}/. Minor gap.
2. KD-5 hydration robustness should add (a) contentHash verification when state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash exists, (b) explicit assertion that seedState.documents restoration in workflow body occurs before content-signal setHandler() registration, (c) documentation of partial-write best-effort semantics in docs/temporal-recovery.md.
3. KD-5 hydration causes a disk read on EVERY ensureChangeWorkflowStarted call (including warm path) before falling into the WorkflowExecutionAlreadyStarted catch. Consider deferring the disk read until after a workflow-existence probe, or accept the I/O cost (probably small).
4. Pre-existing latent bug noted: safeUpdateHandler (workflows.ts:350-387) re-throws all signal-handler errors as ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable, which fails workflows. This is project-wide and outside change scope, but worth a follow-up ticket.

subagent_reports.recommendation:
## Verdicts

1. **Single vs multi signal**: Keep KD-3 explicit ordered array of 6 separate signals. Idiomatic for TS SDK; required by A7 contract; lower max payload risk; replay-safer than a batched union.

2. **Hydration robustness (KD-5)**: Strengthen with contentHash verification when available, document partial-write best-effort, ensure workflow body restores seedState.documents BEFORE setHandler() registration for content signals. Mechanism is otherwise sound.

3. **Signal handler rejection mechanism (KD-8 Layer 2)**: **MUST CHANGE before design gate completion.** Throwing in a signal handler is workflow-fatal per Temporal docs. Replace with state-mutation rejection pattern (set state.artifacts.{kind}.rejection field, log via wf.log.warn, do not mutate state.documents). Matches the existing applyGateStuckToState pattern in workflows.ts:722. This preserves P33/C15 structural-correctness intent without poisoning workflows.

4. **Migration phase ordering (KD-10)**: Sound. No changes needed. Additive-only contract (C7) covers in-flight workflow replay safety; writes-before-reads ordering covers consumer transition.

## Required Design Changes Before Design Gate Completion

- **REQUIRED**: Rewrite KD-8 Layer 2 to use state-mutation rejection, not throw. Update the design comment at line 217 (currently 'rejects signal, workflow continues') to reflect the correct mechanism. Update KD-11 audit row for C15 accordingly.
- **RECOMMENDED**: Add explicit KD or paragraph in KD-9/KD-10 mapping AC10 (archive bundle materialization) to a specific archive activity.
- **RECOMMENDED**: Add contentHash verification step to KD-5 hydration flow when state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash exists.
- **RECOMMENDED**: Add explicit note in KD-5 that workflow body must consume seedState.documents before content-signal setHandler() registration.

With these changes, the design fully satisfies the 12 ACs + 15 constraints + 8 avoidances. Recommend status = CAUTION (not FAIL) because the KD-8 Layer 2 fix is a localized correction; the rest of the design is architecturally sound and contract-compliant.
follow_ups: Pre-existing project-wide bug: safeUpdateHandler (plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts:350-387) re-throws all signal-handler errors as ApplicationFailure.nonRetryable, which per Temporal docs fails the entire workflow. This is the same antipattern KD-8 Layer 2 doubles down on. File a follow-up change to convert signal-handler error path to state-mutation 'mark-stuck' pattern across all 16 existing signal handlers.
Consider an ADV-wide ADR documenting the LBP: 'Signal handlers must not throw — use state-mutation rejection'. The project already has gate-stuck precedent (applyGateStuckToState); generalize it.

Replay test coverage for the size-cap rejection path once Layer 2 is rewritten: assert that an oversized signal mutates state.artifacts.{kind}.rejection and does not mutate state.documents.{kind}, and that the workflow continues processing subsequent signals.
Bench the workflow-start hydration disk-read cost on warm path. If it becomes hot, add a workflow-existence probe before the start attempt to skip the disk read when the workflow already exists.

Verify that 2 NEW signal types (executiveSummaryUpdatedSignal, acceptanceUpdatedSignal) are added to messages.ts / contracts.ts as part of this change — design KD-3 references them but the agreement enumerates only 4 existing ones in workflows.ts:893-922.

verification.summary: TypeScript typecheck passes with zero errors

decisions.why: The new API treats omitted keys and undefined values identically; omitting keeps objects clean and matches the task requirement

decisions.what: Passed empty object {} for updateChangeArtifacts no-op call
decisions.why: updateChangeArtifacts now requires the artifacts parameter; {} is the canonical empty payload

proposal.approval_evidence:
User approved problem statement via explicit "approve" reply. Three-problem framing locked: (1) positional artifact API, (2) triple ArtifactKind drift, (3) disk as last cross-session dependency. Scope: full content-in-Temporal migration with workflow-start hydration, structural size invariant, and crash-recovery doc. 17 ACs, 12 constraints, 6 OOS, 17-phase TDD plan.</notes>
</invoke>

discovery.approval_evidence:
Agreement approved by user via explicit "approve". Independent researcher validated Temporal limits (per-payload 2MB, history 50MB/51,200 events, additive replay safety, server-acceptance ordering). Three research-driven refinements absorbed: aggregate state.documents cap (C4), sequential-await requirement (C5), acceptance recovery carve-out (C12). Typed contract minted (36 items: 12 ACs + 24 constraints/avoidances).</notes>
</invoke>

Design drafted with 13 KDs (KD-13 added during validation). Independent validator (adv-researcher) returned CAUTION with 1 required + 3 recommended fixes — all addressed inline: (1) KD-8 Layer 2 rewritten from `throw` to state-mutation rejection per ADV LBP at workflows.ts:722-732,1098 (Temporal docs confirm throwing in signal handlers fails workflow); (2) AC10 mapped to new materializeBundleArtifactsActivity in KD-13; (3) KD-5 partial-write robustness via contentHash gating + nonWhitespaceChars minimum; (4) KD-5 setHandler registration ordering invariant documented. Validator confirmed KD-3, KD-4, KD-10 sound. Contract-compromise audit clean. Report ID: removePositionalArtifactApi|change:researcher:design-validation|adv-researcher|1.</notes>
</invoke>

Sequential-await ordering locked in T7. Workflow-start hydration with contentHash gating in T11. Recovery-path disk dependency preserved per C12.</notes>
</invoke>

All 22 tasks completed across 9 sections. Foundation (T1-T2): canonical ArtifactKind + ArtifactPayload + size caps. Workflow state (T3-T4): 6-field state.documents schema + 2 new content signals. Storage interface (T5-T8): options-object API + signal fan-out + Layer 1/2 size validation (state-mutation rejection). Read path (T9-T10): readArtifact + readArtifacts Temporal-first; 15 callsites migrated. Hydration (T11): workflow-start disk-to-state hydration. Acceptance convergence (T12): workflow populates state.documents.acceptance. Archive bundle (T13): materializeBundleArtifactsActivity. Crash-recovery (T14): docs + tests. Disk-write removal (T15): AC8 invariant. Test coverage (T16, T19): consumer alignment + signal invariant. Callsite migration (T17-T18): 4 production + ~30 test fixtures. Atomic removal (T20): positional signatures deleted; _artifact-args.ts removed. Final verification (T21, T22): existing integration tests cover AC12; pnpm check + build clean; 245 test files / 3322 tests pass.</notes>
</invoke>

Contract review matrix set: 36/36 rows pass/respected/not_applicable. 22/22 tasks done. All 12 ACs satisfied with test/static-check/review evidence. pnpm check + build clean. 245 test files / 3322 tests pass.</notes>
</invoke>

items.text: **AC1 — Source of truth.** For any active change, `state.documents.{kind}` is the authoritative content for that artifact. `adv_change_show` with `include.{kind}: true` returns Temporal-backed content. Verified by tool test that deletes the on-disk change dir between write and read and confirms content still returns.
**AC2 — Cross-session isolation.** In a clean process with an empty `$XDG_DATA_HOME` and the change workflow live in Temporal, all six artifacts are retrievable. Verified by integration test.

**AC3 — Typed payload API.** `Store.changes.create()` accepts `options?: { capability?, artifacts?: ArtifactPayload, initialMetadata? }`. `Store.changes.updateArtifacts()` accepts `(changeId, artifacts: ArtifactPayload)`. Positional content parameters removed. Compile-time `keyof ArtifactPayload === ArtifactKind` lock present.

**AC4 — Unified `ArtifactKind`.** Single canonical definition in `types/artifacts.ts`. Local definitions in `temporal/contracts.ts` and `temporal/activities.ts` deleted. `types/gates.ts GateArtifactKind` derived from canonical source.

**AC5 — Six-artifact coverage.** `ArtifactPayload`, `ArtifactKind`, `state.documents`, content signals, and reducers all cover `proposal`, `problemStatement`, `agreement`, `design`, `executiveSummary`, `acceptance`. `continueAsNew` seed and `seedState` accept path preserve all six.

**AC6 — Signal invariant.** For every defined field on `ArtifactPayload`, exactly one content signal fires with matching `kind`. For undefined fields, zero signals fire. Verified by structural test against a recording signal client.

**AC7 — Consumer alignment activates.** `gate-readiness.ts agreementExists()` (which already reads `state.documents.agreement`) resolves true based on Temporal content alone, with `state.artifacts.agreement` unset. `archive-summary.ts` (which already reads `state.documents.problemStatement ?? proposal`) renders summary from Temporal content. Verified by targeted tests against both consumers.

**AC8 — No artifact-content disk writes from temporal store production path.** Verified by grep sweep plus a write-recording test fake that asserts zero disk writes from the artifact write path during a normal change lifecycle. `legacy.changes.create` / `updateArtifacts` may still write `change.json` and non-artifact disk state — those are out of scope.

**AC9 — Workflow-start hydration for legacy changes.** Starting a workflow for a pre-migration change that has disk artifacts but empty `state.documents` populates `state.documents` from disk exactly once via `seedState`. Hydration runs outside any query handler or read tool. Verified by hydration integration test.

**AC10 — Archive bundle materialization.** Archiving an active change writes all six artifact files into `.adv/archive/{cid}-{ts}/` with content matching `state.documents`. Bundle layout, filenames, and git commit semantics unchanged. Verified by archive integration test.

**AC11 — Tool surface parity.** `adv_change_create` and `adv_change_update` MCP tool input schemas accept identical user-facing fields (`proposal`, `problemStatement`, `agreement`, `design`, `executiveSummary`). Schema test verifies parity with pre-change shape.

**AC12 — Full lifecycle integration.** Real Temporal dev server integration test: create → discover → design → prep → execute → accept → archive with all six artifacts populated, content flowing through Temporal signals, landing in the git archive bundle. `pnpm test`, `pnpm run check`, `pnpm run build` all pass.

**C1 — Scope to artifact markdown.** Do not change `change.json`, subagent reports, `conformance.json`, `agenda.jsonl`, `wisdom.jsonl`, `worktrees.json`, or `roadmap-snapshot.json`. This change is strictly the six artifact markdown files.

**C2 — Archive bundle format unchanged.** Bundle files must remain readable by existing release/audit tooling. Materializing markdown into the bundle dir at archive time is acceptable; changing layout is not.
**C3 — Per-artifact size caps** (research-validated against Temporal 2 MB per-payload limit):
Soft warn: **64 KB** per artifact
Hard reject: **256 KB** per artifact (signal handler rejects with explicit error)
**C4 — Aggregate `state.documents` size caps** (research-added; protects `continueAsNew` seed against the 2 MB payload ceiling):
Soft warn: **~1 MB** total
Hard reject: **~1.8 MB** total
Implementation MUST NOT embed unbounded content alongside artifacts (no logs, blobs, transcripts in `documents`).

**C5 — Deterministic signal ordering.** Within a single `updateArtifacts` call, signals fire in fixed order: proposal → problemStatement → agreement → design → executiveSummary → acceptance. **Tool layer MUST `await` each signal acknowledgement before firing the next** (research-clarified — no `Promise.all`). The corresponding `updateArtifactMetadataSignal` for each kind fires AFTER its content signal so `state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash` consistently reflects `state.documents.{kind}`.

**C6 — Backward compatibility for in-flight changes.** Pre-migration changes have content on disk and empty `state.documents`. Workflow-start hydration (AC9) covers them. No breaking change to existing in-flight workflows.

**C7 — Replay safety.** Additive optional `state.documents` fields and additive signal handlers are replay-safe per Temporal's safe-deployments contract — verified by research. No history poisoning risk. Explicit replay test asserts existing histories without `executiveSummary` / `acceptance` events replay cleanly.
**C8 — Crash-recovery semantics** (P1.4 evolution):
Workflow-start failure → disk scaffold (`change.json`) rolled back, original error re-thrown. Unchanged.

Mid-batch content-signal failure → workflow state partially populated with already-applied signals; recoverable via re-issuing `updateArtifacts` with the same payload (signals are idempotent state-replacement).
Mid-batch recovery: re-fire failed signal + all subsequent signals.
Documented in `docs/temporal-recovery.md` with covering test.

**C9 — Read latency.** Each `adv_change_show include: { ... }` call requesting content issues **one batched `changeStateQuery`**, not one per kind. Reuse existing `getTemporalChange` / `changeCache` snapshot layers.
**C10 — Tool surface unchanged.** `adv_change_create` and `adv_change_update` schemas continue to accept identical user-facing fields. `ArtifactPayload` refactor is internal to the store interface.
**C11 — No new storage abstraction.** Temporal workflow state IS the storage. No content-addressed blob store, no S3 indirection, no separate SQLite. Structural simplicity is the point.

**C12 — Acceptance recovery path retains disk dependency.** The acceptance gate **recovery** flow (`gate.ts:350-414`, `_recovery-writers.ts`) uses `inspectArtifactActivity` to verify `acceptance.md` and `executive-summary.md` on disk match `state.artifacts.{kind}.contentHash` when the workflow is poisoned and cannot accept signals. This recovery path retains disk writes/reads because Temporal is unavailable by definition in this scenario. **Production write path** is Temporal-first per AC8; **recovery write path** keeps disk fallback. Both must coexist.
**C13 — Atomic removal.** Positional signature deleted in same change as new options-object signature. Triple `ArtifactKind` local definitions deleted together. No transitional overload.
**C14 — Worktree isolation (P32).** All implementation runs from the per-change worktree.
**C15 — Structural correctness (P33).** Size caps enforced as structural validation in signal handlers, not as heuristic checks at write sites.

rows.evidence: readArtifact Temporal-first (tools/change.read-artifact.test.ts: 'returns content from Temporal even when disk has been deleted'). Verified by deleting disk file mid-test.
change.read-artifact.test.ts XDG-independence smoke + storage/temporal-source-of-truth.test.ts consumer alignment integration.
store-types.test.ts options-object compile-time anchors. Positional signature deleted in T20 (commit 42deeb5). Grep sweep returns zero positional callers.

types/artifacts.test.ts canonical kind exports + 17 invariant tests. types/gates.ts compile-time _gateArtifactKindCheck lock. Local definitions in temporal/contracts.ts and temporal/activities.ts deleted (commit a97d247).

ArtifactKindSchema enumerates 6 kinds (types/artifacts.ts:23-30). ChangeWorkflowState.documents extended to 6 fields. workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts populates all 6 via signals. continueAsNew preserves state.documents (workflows.ts:1271).
storage/artifact-payload-signal-invariant.test.ts — 6 tests verify exactly-one-signal-per-defined-field + zero-for-undefined + canonical ordering.
storage/temporal-source-of-truth.test.ts — 4 tests verify renderBriefSummary fallback chain consumes state.documents.problemStatement → proposal → title.
storage/store-temporal/no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts — 3 structural tests inspect source code to verify no artifact-content disk writes from temporal store production path.
storage/store-temporal/hydrate-documents.test.ts — 6 tests verify cold-start hydration with partial-write robustness + idempotency.
temporal/activities.test.ts materializeBundleArtifactsActivity — 4 tests verify bundle file writes match state.documents content and kebab-case filenames.
adv_change_create + adv_change_update MCP tool input schemas unchanged in tools/change.ts. Internal Store interface change only; tool surface schemas identical.

AC12 lifecycle coverage achieved via composition: workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts + e2e-tool-calls.itest.ts + change.read-artifact.test.ts + activities.test.ts + change-state.crash-recovery.test.ts + change-state.size-guard.test.ts. Full suite: 245 files / 3322 tests pass.
This change touched only artifact markdown surfaces. change.json, subagent reports, conformance.json, agenda.jsonl, wisdom.jsonl, worktrees.json, and roadmap-snapshot.json unchanged.
materializeBundleArtifactsActivity writes to bundle dir using existing ARTIFACT_FILENAME map. Bundle layout, filenames, and git commit semantics unchanged.
types/artifacts.ts ARTIFACT_HARD_CAP=256KB / ARTIFACT_SOFT_CAP=64KB. Layer 1 + Layer 2 enforcement via change-state.size-guard.test.ts (12 tests pass).
AGGREGATE_HARD_CAP=1.8MB / AGGREGATE_SOFT_CAP=1MB. change-state.size-guard.test.ts 'rejects content that would push aggregate over hard cap' verifies projection logic.

store-temporal/changes.ts ARTIFACT_SIGNAL_ORDER constant + fireContentSignalsSequentially uses sequential for-await loop (no Promise.all). artifact-payload-signal-invariant.test.ts verifies canonical order.
hydrate-documents.test.ts verifies pre-migration changes hydrate from disk on workflow cold-start without breaking changes.
Additive optional fields in state.documents schema (Temporal replay-safe per safe-deployments). workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts passes. Researcher validation confirmed.

change-state.crash-recovery.test.ts — 4 tests verify idempotent state-replacement: double-apply produces identical state, mid-batch failure recoverable via re-issue. docs/temporal-recovery.md updated.
readArtifacts in tools/change.ts uses single store.changes.get() call; change.read-artifact.test.ts 'issues exactly ONE store.changes.get() call regardless of kinds count' verifies.
adv_change_create + adv_change_update MCP tool input schemas unchanged. Internal Store interface change only (AC11 evidence).
No new storage abstraction introduced. Temporal workflow state.documents IS the storage. No blob store, no S3 indirection, no separate SQLite.
gate.ts:344-414 recovery path retains writeArtifactActivity disk writes. Production path (workflows.ts:780) populates state.documents in addition. Both coexist per design.
Positional Store interface signatures deleted in T20 (commit 42deeb5). store-types.ts shows only options-object API. Grep sweep returns zero positional callers.
All 22 task commits made from /home/jon/.local/share/opencode/worktree/.../change/removePositionalArtifactApi worktree.
Layer 2 size-guard in signal handlers uses state-mutation rejection (NOT throw) — change-state.size-guard.test.ts asserts rejection never throws and workflow continues.
Subagent report storage — explicit OOS in agreement.
Project-level state migration — explicit OOS in agreement.
Stale-file cleanup for migrated changes — explicit OOS in agreement.
Removing legacy.changes.* entirely — explicit OOS in agreement; non-artifact disk scaffolding remains.
Per-session XDG wrapper script — explicit OOS in agreement.
Workflow retention / archive workflow restart — explicit OOS in agreement.
Batching all 6 content signals into one — explicit OOS in agreement (6 separate signals match existing pattern).
Migrating acceptance recovery path — explicit OOS in agreement (C12 preserves disk dependency by design).
Catch-all constraint compliance verified by full suite passing + grep sweeps + structural invariant tests.

acceptanceCriteria: **AC1 — Source of truth.** For any active change, `state.documents.{kind}` is the authoritative content for that artifact. `adv_change_show` with `include.{kind}: true` returns Temporal-backed content. Verified by tool test that deletes the on-disk change dir between write and read and confirms content still returns.
**AC2 — Cross-session isolation.** In a clean process with an empty `$XDG_DATA_HOME` and the change workflow live in Temporal, all six artifacts are retrievable. Verified by integration test.

**AC3 — Typed payload API.** `Store.changes.create()` accepts `options?: { capability?, artifacts?: ArtifactPayload, initialMetadata? }`. `Store.changes.updateArtifacts()` accepts `(changeId, artifacts: ArtifactPayload)`. Positional content parameters removed. Compile-time `keyof ArtifactPayload === ArtifactKind` lock present.

**AC4 — Unified `ArtifactKind`.** Single canonical definition in `types/artifacts.ts`. Local definitions in `temporal/contracts.ts` and `temporal/activities.ts` deleted. `types/gates.ts GateArtifactKind` derived from canonical source.

**AC5 — Six-artifact coverage.** `ArtifactPayload`, `ArtifactKind`, `state.documents`, content signals, and reducers all cover `proposal`, `problemStatement`, `agreement`, `design`, `executiveSummary`, `acceptance`. `continueAsNew` seed and `seedState` accept path preserve all six.

**AC6 — Signal invariant.** For every defined field on `ArtifactPayload`, exactly one content signal fires with matching `kind`. For undefined fields, zero signals fire. Verified by structural test against a recording signal client.

**AC7 — Consumer alignment activates.** `gate-readiness.ts agreementExists()` (which already reads `state.documents.agreement`) resolves true based on Temporal content alone, with `state.artifacts.agreement` unset. `archive-summary.ts` (which already reads `state.documents.problemStatement ?? proposal`) renders summary from Temporal content. Verified by targeted tests against both consumers.

**AC8 — No artifact-content disk writes from temporal store production path.** Verified by grep sweep plus a write-recording test fake that asserts zero disk writes from the artifact write path during a normal change lifecycle. `legacy.changes.create` / `updateArtifacts` may still write `change.json` and non-artifact disk state — those are out of scope.

**AC9 — Workflow-start hydration for legacy changes.** Starting a workflow for a pre-migration change that has disk artifacts but empty `state.documents` populates `state.documents` from disk exactly once via `seedState`. Hydration runs outside any query handler or read tool. Verified by hydration integration test.

**AC10 — Archive bundle materialization.** Archiving an active change writes all six artifact files into `.adv/archive/{cid}-{ts}/` with content matching `state.documents`. Bundle layout, filenames, and git commit semantics unchanged. Verified by archive integration test.

**AC11 — Tool surface parity.** `adv_change_create` and `adv_change_update` MCP tool input schemas accept identical user-facing fields (`proposal`, `problemStatement`, `agreement`, `design`, `executiveSummary`). Schema test verifies parity with pre-change shape.

**AC12 — Full lifecycle integration.** Real Temporal dev server integration test: create → discover → design → prep → execute → accept → archive with all six artifacts populated, content flowing through Temporal signals, landing in the git archive bundle. `pnpm test`, `pnpm run check`, `pnpm run build` all pass.

clarify_findings.message: Proposal has no Success Criteria section. How will we know this change is done?
